## Steps to Reproduce
Proccess this code with `ktlint --format`:

```
package com.my.lint.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

class BreakKtlintFormatRule : Rule(
    RuleId("my-ruleset:my-rule-id"),
    Rule.About(maintainer = "Me", repositoryUrl = "repositoryUrl", issueTrackerUrl = "issueTrackerUrl")
) {

    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
        node: ASTNode,
        autoCorrect: Boolean,
        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit
    ) {

        if (node.text.contains("this")
            || node.text.contains("that")
        ) {
            emit(node.startOffset, "My violation detail", false)
        }
    }
}
```

* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Command line
* Version of Gradle used (if applicable): N/A
* Operating System and version: macOS 13.2

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

content.text:
All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary

### Root Cause
The issue was in the `isElvisOperator()` function in `IndentationRule.kt` (line 1091-1094). The function was checking if a node is an Elvis operator (`?:`), but it was accessing `firstChildNode.elementType` without first checking if `firstChildNode` is null.

When Kotlin code has a line starting with `||` (OR operator), the parser creates an `OPERATION_REFERENCE` node that may have no children, causing `firstChildNode` to be null. Accessing `.elementType` on null causes the `NullPointerException` seen in the error.

### Fix
Changed the unsafe access:
```kotlin
firstChildNode.elementType == ELVIS
```
to a null-safe access:
```kotlin
firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
```

This simple one-character change (`?` after `firstChildNode`) ensures that when `firstChildNode` is null, the expression evaluates to `null == ELVIS` which is `false`, instead of throwing a NullPointerException.

### Files Modified
1. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`** - Added null-safe operator to fix the crash

### Files Created
1. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleOrOperatorOnNewLineTest.kt`** - New test file with test cases for the specific issue
